meta.title: Query Processing and Optimization
meta.subtitle: Making Generated Columns for SARGABLE Query Optimization

paragraphs.text: This project demonstrates query optimization techniques using generated columns in MySQL to create SARGABLE (Search ARGument ABLE) queries. The study uses a realistic scenario of a jewelry store conducting a birthday-month promotion campaign, showing how to efficiently identify customers born in a specific month.

By implementing generated columns and optimizing query predicates, we achieve significant performance improvements while maintaining data accuracy and consistency through automation. The project compares traditional unoptimized queries against SARGABLE optimized queries.

items.secondary: Jewelry store owner wants to run a birthday-month promotion campaign
How to verify customer information and identify customers born in a specific month
Send targeted promotional offers to customers with birthdays in the selected month
Implement generated columns for efficient query processing

tabsContent.title: Dataset Overview
tabsContent.description: Synthetic customer dataset created for demonstration purposes, loaded into MySQL using bulk data loading.
tabsContent.note: Note: birth_month is a generated column extracted from date_of_birth for query optimization

sections.heading: What Are Generated Columns?
sections.body: Generated columns are calculated from other columns in the same row. They automatically compute values based on expressions applied to other columns, eliminating the need for manual calculations and reducing errors.

definitions.description: Computed when queried, not stored in the table. Saves storage space but requires computation on each query.
Computed once and saved in the table. Uses more storage but improves query performance and enables indexing.

items.label: Approach
items.value: Extract birth month on-the-fly using MONTH() function on date_of_birth

items.label: Complexity
items.value: O(n) - Must scan ALL rows and compute MONTH() for each

items.label: Approach
items.value: Use SARGABLE generated column (birth_month) with indexed lookup

items.label: Complexity
items.value: O(log n + k) - Index lookup plus fetch matching rows

content.text: SARGABLE (Search ARGument ABLE) predicates enable the query optimizer to use indexes efficiently. By moving the calculation into a generated column and indexing it, the database can use index range scans instead of full table scans.

items.label: Without Optimization
items.value: Full Table Scan - examines every single row to calculate the birth month

items.label: With Optimization
items.value: Index Range Scan - uses the index on birth_month to locate only matching rows

content.text: Reduces logical reads by approximately 15x. While 1.4 seconds may seem small with current dataset size, the improvement scales dramatically as data grows to millions of rows.
Every customer added to the database automatically gets the birth month calculated. The database engine ensures 100% data accuracy without manual intervention.
Application code can simply query the birth_month column directly instead of performing complex date calculations. This reduces code complexity and maintenance overhead.
Generated columns ensure consistent calculations across all queries and applications accessing the same table. No risk of calculation discrepancies.
Creating additional columns and indexes consumes more disk space. However, the performance gain typically outweighs this cost.
Once defined, changing the generation expression requires schema alterations. This can be complex in production environments with continuous data flow.
While queries become faster, INSERT and UPDATE operations might be slightly slower due to the computation of generated values. This is a worthwhile trade-off for read-heavy applications.

tabsContent.title: Research Team - Query Slaves
tabsContent.footerNote: Team Name: "Query Slaves" - Dedicated to optimizing database queries and performance
